old_claim.note: Early Electrical Appliances is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim.note: The scoped electric washer required a small electric motor to drive the drum and reversing mechanism.

source_shape.source_locator: Patent specification describes a motor-operated drive mechanism for washing machines, the cylinder operating in a wash-water tub, reversible gearing, and pulley drive from a small electric motor.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The Fisher/Hurley patent makes the electric motor a direct component in the early electric washer's drum and reversing drive.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The old edge pointed to the broad class of early appliances, but the patent identifies the specific mechanism needed for the scoped node: a motor driving the washer cylinder through belts and reversing gears.

ontology_before: Mechanized Laundry depended on Early Electrical Appliances as a broad historical predecessor, implying a category-to-category lineage rather than a direct mechanism.
ontology_after: Mechanized Laundry is scoped to early electric washers and depends directly on electric_motor as the component that powers the drum and reversing drive.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: mechanized_laundry now depends directly on electric_motor and no longer directly depends on electrical_appliances_early.
would_reject_if: The node is later broadened back to hand-cranked, steam, or institutional laundry systems that do not require electric motors.
A stronger source shows the 1907 Thor-style washer did not use an electric motor for its washing mechanism.
A more specific electric_washing_machine node is added and this broad node is moved to general laundry mechanization.
short_reason: The source-backed direct mechanism is the electric motor, not the broad early-appliance category.
